message:
feat(ci): add type-fork detection script for franken_kernel canonicals (bd-1usdh.3)

scripts/check_type_forks.sh scans all Rust sources for competing
definitions of canonical types (TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId, etc).
Supports --json output for CI. Baseline of 7 known pre-migration forks
documented in .type_fork_baseline.json.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1usdh","title":"[FOUNDATION] franken_kernel crate — Suite-Wide Type Substrate","description":"Implement the franken_kernel crate that defines canonical types used across ALL FrankenSuite projects: TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId, SchemaVersion, and the Cx type. No other crate may define competing versions. This is the suite workspace architecture's foundation — dependency firewall rule: /dp/* crates depend on franken_kernel, not the reverse. Build-time enforcement: CI fails if any crate defines forked types.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:51.516645669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:51.516645669Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","franken-kernel","types"]}
 {"id":"bd-1usdh.1","title":"[FOUNDATION] Define canonical TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId, SchemaVersion types","description":"Define the canonical identifier and version types for the franken_kernel crate. These types are used across ALL FrankenSuite projects for cross-project tracing, decision logging, and schema compatibility. TraceId: 128-bit unique identifier (UUIDv7 format for time-ordered generation), implements Display, FromStr, Serialize, Deserialize, Hash, Eq, Ord. DecisionId: 128-bit identifier linking a runtime decision to its EvidenceLedger entry, same trait implementations. PolicyId: identifies a decision policy (scheduler, cancellation, budget), includes version for policy evolution tracking. SchemaVersion: semantic version (major.minor.patch) with compatibility checking -- SchemaVersion::is_compatible(other) returns true iff major versions match. All types must be: (1) Copy (128-bit fits in registers), (2) Send + Sync (safe for cross-thread use), (3) no_std compatible (usable in embedded contexts), (4) Zero-cost abstraction over [u8; 16]. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: Span: franken_kernel::type_init (one-time at crate init). DEBUG: type constructed with value -- emitted only when debug_assertions enabled. WARN: SchemaVersion::is_compatible() returned false -- signals schema migration needed. INFO: TraceId::new() at trace propagation boundaries only. Metrics: trace_id_generation_rate (counter), schema_version_compat_check_total (counter), schema_version_compat_failure_total (counter). All metrics exposed via metrics crate with prometheus-compatible naming.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:22:00.814450673Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T19:00:36.475213355Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T19:00:36.475079978Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","franken-kernel","types"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1usdh.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:36Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1usdh.2","title":"[FOUNDATION] Define canonical Cx type for suite-wide use","description":"Define the canonical Cx (capability context) type in franken_kernel for suite-wide use. Cx is the core abstraction that threads through all Asupersync operations -- it carries capabilities, budget, deadline, trace context, and evidence ledger reference. The franken_kernel Cx must be: (1) Generic over capability set -- Cx<C: CapabilitySet> so each project defines its own capabilities, (2) Lifetime-bound -- Cx<'a, C> ensures capabilities cannot outlive their scope, (3) Propagation-aware -- child Cx inherits parent capabilities (attenuated via Macaroons), (4) Budget-aware -- carries remaining budget in tropical semiring (min, +), (5) Trace-aware -- carries TraceId for cross-project distributed tracing. Must define the CapabilitySet trait with methods: has<T>(), get<T>(), attenuate<T>(caveat). This is the most critical type in the entire FrankenSuite. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: Span: cx::new with parent_trace_id, budget_remaining_ms, capability_count. Span: cx::propagate when creating child Cx from parent. DEBUG: Cx<C> created with trace_id={id}, budget={budget_ms}ms, capabilities=[{cap_names}]. DEBUG: Cx attenuated: capability={cap_name}, caveat={caveat_desc}. INFO: Cx budget exhausted: trace_id={id}, original_budget={orig}ms, elapsed={elapsed}ms -- emitted once per exhaustion event. WARN: Cx capability check failed: trace_id={id}, required={cap_name}, available=[{available_caps}]. Metrics: cx_creation_total (counter), cx_propagation_total (counter), cx_budget_exhaustion_total (counter), cx_capability_check_total (counter by result=granted|denied), cx_budget_remaining_ms (histogram at propagation boundaries).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:22:12.440993822Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T19:28:30.247627068Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T19:28:30.247495884Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cx","foundation","franken-kernel"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1usdh.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:39Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1usdh.3","title":"[FOUNDATION] Implement build-time enforcement for type uniqueness","description":"Implement build-time enforcement that prevents any FrankenSuite crate from defining types that compete with franken_kernel canonical types. This prevents the forked types anti-pattern where multiple crates define their own TraceId, leading to conversion overhead and confusion. Implementation approaches: (1) CI lint: A custom clippy-like lint (or script) that scans all crate sources for struct definitions named TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId, SchemaVersion, or Cx, and fails if found outside franken_kernel, (2) Workspace-level cargo deny: Use cargo-deny to enforce that only franken_kernel exports these type names, (3) Documentation: Add clear comments in franken_kernel explaining that these types are canonical and MUST NOT be forked. Acceptance criteria: A PR that adds struct TraceId to any crate other than franken_kernel is rejected by CI with a clear error message. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: INFO: type uniqueness check passed -- scanned {crate_count} crates, found 0 forks. ERROR: type fork detected in crate {crate_name}: found competing definition of {type_name} at {file}:{line}. WARN: type re-export detected in crate {crate_name} -- re-exports are allowed but should use pub use franken_kernel::{type_name}. Metrics: type_fork_scan_duration_ms (histogram), type_fork_count (gauge, target: 0), crates_scanned_total (counter). CI integration: scan runs as a pre-merge check, logs results as structured JSON to CI artifact directory.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:22:23.529057799Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:00.836990578Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","enforcement","foundation","franken-kernel"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1usdh.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1usdh.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1usdh.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:10Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1usdh.4","title":"[FOUNDATION] Unit and integration tests for franken_kernel crate","description":"Comprehensive test suite for the franken_kernel crate (TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId, SchemaVersion, Cx type).

**Unit tests:**
(1) TraceId UUIDv7 ordering — generate 10,000 TraceIds in sequence; verify they are monotonically ordered (UUIDv7 encodes timestamp in the high bits).
(2) TraceId roundtrip — Display -> FromStr roundtrip for random TraceIds; verify equality. Also test Serialize -> Deserialize roundtrip.
(3) DecisionId/PolicyId parity — same roundtrip and ordering tests as TraceId (shared underlying [u8; 16] representation).
(4) SchemaVersion compatibility — verify is_compatible() returns true for same major version, false for different major version. Test edge cases: 0.x vs 0.y, pre-release versions.
(5) SchemaVersion ordering — verify Ord implementation matches semver ordering (1.0.0 < 1.0.1 < 1.1.0 < 2.0.0).
(6) Copy semantics — verify all types are Copy by passing by value twice without clone (compile-time check via trybuild).
(7) Send + Sync — verify all types are Send + Sync (compile-time check).
(8) no_std compatibility — verify crate compiles with no_std (conditional compilation test).
(9) Cx generic capability set — verify Cx<C> works with custom CapabilitySet implementations; test has<T>(), get<T>(), attenuate().
(10) Cx lifetime safety — verify that Cx<'a, C> cannot outlive its scope (compile-fail test via trybuild).
(11) Cx budget propagation — verify that child Cx inherits parent budget in tropical semiring (min, +); test budget exhaustion.
(12) Cx trace propagation — verify that child Cx inherits parent TraceId for distributed tracing.

**Integration tests:**
(13) Cross-crate type uniqueness — create a mock workspace with a crate that imports franken_kernel types; verify the types are usable and no forking occurs.
(14) Cx capability attenuation chain — create a capability, attenuate it 10 times with increasingly restrictive caveats; verify that authority monotonically decreases.
(15) Cx with EvidenceLedger — verify that Cx carries an evidence_ledger reference and entries can be emitted through it.

**Property-based tests (proptest):**
(16) All identifier types: Hash consistency — if a == b then hash(a) == hash(b) for random pairs.
(17) SchemaVersion: transitivity of is_compatible().
(18) Cx budget: tropical semiring laws (associativity of min, commutativity of min, identity element).

**Logging requirements:**
- Each test logs: test_name, type_under_test, assertion_count, execution_time_us.
- On failure, log the specific input that caused the assertion failure with full Debug output.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- All roundtrip properties hold for 10,000 random inputs.
- All compile-fail tests produce clear, understandable error messages.
- Tropical semiring laws hold for 100,000 random budget values.
- Cx lifetime safety is enforced at compile time (no runtime checks needed).

**Rust implementation notes:**
- Use proptest for all random generation with custom strategies for each type.
- Use trybuild for compile-fail and compile-pass tests.
- Use tempfile for no_std compilation tests (cargo build with custom target).
- Tests must be runnable with both std and no_std configurations.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:22:11.959293410Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:22:42.941834446Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","franken-kernel","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1usdh.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:43Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1usdh.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:43Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1usdh.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:43Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1usdh.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:43Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1v4tc","title":"[LEAN-COVERAGE][T1] Inventory non-negotiable invariants and formalization status","description":"Create a full inventory of Asupersync non-negotiable invariants and map each to Lean definitions/theorems and executable tests.

Scope:
- Structured concurrency ownership.
- Region close => quiescence.
- Cancellation protocol semantics.
- Loser-drain obligations.
- No obligation leaks / explicit capability discipline.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Every invariant has a formal status: fully proven / partially proven / unproven.
- Each invariant includes links to runtime conformance tests.
- Missing invariants spawn explicit follow-up obligations.","notes":"Claimed after closing bd-3n3b2/bd-2un63/bd-1drgu.\n\n[2026-02-11 MagentaBridge] Completed invariant inventory + formalization status mapping.\n\nArtifacts added:\n- formal/lean/coverage/invariant_status_inventory.json\n- tests/lean_invariant_status_inventory.rs\n\nWhat was delivered:\n1) Enumerated all non-negotiable invariants and assigned explicit Lean formalization status: fully_proven / partially_proven / unproven.\n2) For each invariant, linked theorem witnesses (lean_theorems) and executable runtime checks (test_refs).\n3) Recorded concrete known gaps and spawned/linked follow-up obligations via follow_up_beads where coverage is partial/unproven.\n4) Added consistency validation test to enforce schema integrity and prevent stale references.\n\nValidation evidence:\n- cargo test --test lean_invariant_status_inventory -- --nocapture (pass)\n- Full project gates previously run during this slice: fmt/check/clippy/test all green in latest pass.\n\nAcceptance criteria mapping:\n- Every invariant has formal status: satisfied.\n- Each invariant includes runtime test links: satisfied.\n- Missing invariant proof coverage has explicit follow-up obligations: satisfied.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaBridge","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:16:10.057792187Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T17:05:33.704165438Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T17:05:33.704099456Z","close_reason":"Completed invariant inventory, status classification, theorem/test linkage, and follow-up obligations.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1v4tc","depends_on_id":"bd-13aa6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:50Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1v4tc","depends_on_id":"bd-3kzbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:50Z","created_by":"import"}]}

Include unit tests for interceptor ordering, metadata propagation, and cancellation behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:44:03.666304121Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T06:49:52.579258196Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T06:49:52.579187795Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["grpc","middleware"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1v6a","depends_on_id":"bd-1q7p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:47Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1v6a","depends_on_id":"bd-1vz0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:47Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1v6a","depends_on_id":"bd-n97c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:47Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1v6a","depends_on_id":"bd-qe1u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:47Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1v6a","depends_on_id":"bd-z1w4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:47Z","created_by":"import"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+{
+  "_comment": "Known pre-migration type forks. These existed before franken_kernel was created and will be resolved by future migration beads. New forks MUST NOT be added.",
+  "baseline_count": 7,
+  "known_forks": [
+    {"file": "src/types/budget.rs", "type": "Budget"},
+    {"file": "src/types/task_context.rs", "type": "Cx"},
+    {"file": "src/cx/cx.rs", "type": "Cx"},
+    {"file": "src/trace/distributed/id.rs", "type": "TraceId"},
+    {"file": "conformance/src/tests/budget.rs", "type": "Budget"},
+    {"file": "asupersync_v4_api_skeleton.rs", "type": "Budget"},
+    {"file": "asupersync_v4_api_skeleton.rs", "type": "Cx"}
+  ]
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,121 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# check_type_forks.sh — Enforce franken_kernel type uniqueness (bd-1usdh.3).
+#
+# Scans all Rust source files for competing definitions of canonical types
+# that must only be defined in franken_kernel. Exits non-zero if forks found.
+#
+# Usage: scripts/check_type_forks.sh [--json]
+#
+# Exit codes:
+#   0 — no forks detected
+#   1 — type forks detected
+#   2 — usage error
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
+
+# Canonical types that MUST only be defined in franken_kernel.
+CANONICAL_TYPES=(
+    "TraceId"
+    "DecisionId"
+    "PolicyId"
+    "SchemaVersion"
+    "Budget"
+    "NoCaps"
+)
+
+# Also check Cx but it needs special handling (common word in other contexts).
+CX_PATTERN="^pub struct Cx"
+
+# The one crate allowed to define these types.
+ALLOWED_CRATE="franken_kernel"
+
+JSON_MODE=false
+if [[ "${1:-}" == "--json" ]]; then
+    JSON_MODE=true
+fi
+
+fork_count=0
+crates_scanned=0
+forks=()
+
+# Find all Rust source files, excluding franken_kernel and target dirs.
+while IFS= read -r src_file; do
+    # Determine which crate this file belongs to.
+    rel_path="${src_file#"$REPO_ROOT/"}"
+    crate_dir="${rel_path%%/*}"
+
+    # Skip the allowed crate.
+    if [[ "$crate_dir" == "$ALLOWED_CRATE" ]]; then
+        continue
+    fi
+
+    # Skip target directories and hidden directories.
+    if [[ "$rel_path" == target/* ]] || [[ "$rel_path" == .* ]]; then
+        continue
+    fi
+
+    crates_scanned=$((crates_scanned + 1))
+
+    # Check for struct/enum/type alias definitions of canonical types.
+    for type_name in "${CANONICAL_TYPES[@]}"; do
+        # Match: pub struct TypeName, struct TypeName, pub enum TypeName,
+        #        pub type TypeName, type TypeName
+        pattern="^[[:space:]]*(pub[[:space:]]+)?(struct|enum|type)[[:space:]]+${type_name}[^a-zA-Z0-9_]"
+
+        if grep -nE "$pattern" "$src_file" 2>/dev/null | grep -v "^[[:space:]]*//" | grep -v "^[[:space:]]*\*" > /dev/null; then
+            line_info=$(grep -nE "$pattern" "$src_file" 2>/dev/null | grep -v "^[[:space:]]*//" | grep -v "^[[:space:]]*\*" | head -1)
+            line_num="${line_info%%:*}"
+            fork_count=$((fork_count + 1))
+            forks+=("${rel_path}:${line_num}:${type_name}")
+
+            if [[ "$JSON_MODE" == false ]]; then
+                echo "ERROR: type fork detected — ${type_name} defined in ${rel_path}:${line_num}"
+                echo "       Should use: use franken_kernel::${type_name};"
+            fi
+        fi
+    done
+
+    # Special check for Cx.
+    if grep -nE "$CX_PATTERN" "$src_file" 2>/dev/null | grep -v "^[[:space:]]*//" > /dev/null; then
+        line_info=$(grep -nE "$CX_PATTERN" "$src_file" 2>/dev/null | grep -v "^[[:space:]]*//" | head -1)
+        line_num="${line_info%%:*}"
+        fork_count=$((fork_count + 1))
+        forks+=("${rel_path}:${line_num}:Cx")
+
+        if [[ "$JSON_MODE" == false ]]; then
+            echo "ERROR: type fork detected — Cx defined in ${rel_path}:${line_num}"
+            echo "       Should use: use franken_kernel::Cx;"
+        fi
+    fi
+done < <(find "$REPO_ROOT" -name "*.rs" -not -path "*/target/*" -not -path "*/.git/*" -type f 2>/dev/null)
+
+if [[ "$JSON_MODE" == true ]]; then
+    # Output structured JSON.
+    forks_json="["
+    for i in "${!forks[@]}"; do
+        IFS=: read -r file line type_name <<< "${forks[$i]}"
+        if [[ $i -gt 0 ]]; then forks_json+=","; fi
+        forks_json+="{\"file\":\"${file}\",\"line\":${line},\"type\":\"${type_name}\"}"
+    done
+    forks_json+="]"
+
+    cat <<EOJSON
+{
+  "type_fork_count": ${fork_count},
+  "crates_scanned": ${crates_scanned},
+  "forks": ${forks_json},
+  "status": "$([ "$fork_count" -eq 0 ] && echo "pass" || echo "fail")"
+}
+EOJSON
+else
+    echo ""
+    if [[ "$fork_count" -eq 0 ]]; then
+        echo "INFO: type uniqueness check passed — scanned ${crates_scanned} files, found 0 forks."
+    else
+        echo "FAIL: found ${fork_count} type fork(s) across ${crates_scanned} files."
+        echo "      All canonical types must be imported from franken_kernel."
+        exit 1
+    fi
+fi
